tasks.prompt: Our pure vector search keeps missing exact matches on product SKUs and part numbers, and part of our catalog is in German. We want to add a keyword-style signal alongside the embeddings. Which kind of sparse/lexical representation should we use, and what do we need to watch out for given the SKUs and the non-English text?

rubric.text: Recommends adding a sparse vector for lexical/exact matching alongside the dense vector (payload filtering gives no ranking score)
Suggests BM25 as the baseline sparse option (built-in, server-side, works out-of-domain)
Flags that tokenization/stemming config critically affects exact matches on SKUs / part numbers
Addresses German content — BM25 must be configured per language; several learned options (BM42/miniCOIL/SPLADE FastEmbed) are English-only
Mentions named vectors must be configured at collection creation (can't be added later)
Notes alternatives (SPLADE++ term expansion, external bge-m3 for multilingual) with tradeoffs
Recommends a sparse model without evaluating quality/resources on the user's own data
Proposes payload filtering as the ranking solution for keyword relevance

tasks.prompt: I'm having serious problems with a Hybrid RAG search (on texts 100% on Spanish). The user search for "estafa piramidal" (ponzy scam), and it's finding a document what doesn't contain "estafa" nor "piramidal", but it contains A LOT of "esta" (for example: estado, estaban, estatuto, estarían, éstas, and other). I'm about to do a deep debug, trying with only dense vector, and then with only bm25, and see what happens. But, after asking in a couple of different AI, the answer is always the same: "The BM25 may not be properly creating the tokens for Spanish". I wonder if that could be a problem on Qdrant. I'm using the internal BM25. I'm using embedding-small-3. I'm analyzing if moving to large-3 would improve it, but seems like it shouldn't create a big different.

rubric.text: Confirms this is a BM25 language configuration issue rather than a Qdrant bug - by default BM25 is not configured for Spanish tokenization/stemming
Provides the concrete fix: setting tokenizer to 'multilingual' and language to 'spanish' in the BM25 vector options at upsert time
Notes that avg_len should be set to an estimate of the average length of the actual texts, rather than left at a mismatched default
Notes that both the indexed documents and the query-time search must use matching BM25 language/tokenizer configuration for the fix to work
Attributes the bug to embedding model choice (e.g. suggests switching from embedding-small-3 to large-3) as the fix, since the issue is in the sparse/BM25 leg, not the dense embedding
Recommends re-architecting the fusion method (RRF vs DBSF) as the fix, since the root cause is upstream in tokenization, not in how results are combined
